game_info.title: PhysiType
game_info.concept:
How does a p fall?

App Store URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/supertype/id1349292121

================================================================================
Full Description:
================================================================================

How does a p fall?
How much does an r weigh?
And how does the dot over an i sound?

· · · s u p e r t y p e · · ·

(super many, over 130 levels)
(super relaxing, zen-like gameplay)
(super smooth, self-generating sound track)
game_info.description:
PhysiType is a physics-based puzzle game where your words become physical objects.
The goal of each level is to collect all the shining target dots.
You do this by typing letters into the spawn line. Each letter has unique physical properties—an 'o' will roll, an 'l' is a solid beam, and a 'p' is top-heavy.
Plan your text carefully, position spaces to adjust falling points, and press ENTER to bring your letters to life!
game_info.controls:
A-Z: Type letters to spawn
SPACE: Add spacing between letters
BACKSPACE: Remove last letter
ENTER: Start simulation (turn text into physics bodies)
R: Reset level (if simulation fails) / Restart Game (at Game Over)
ESC: Pause / Unpause
ARROW KEYS: Pan camera (if zoomed in or large level)

TEST_1.test_description: Verify that a simple vertical drop works.
TEST_1.strategy_description: On Level 1 (target directly below spawn), type the letter 'l' and start simulation. Wait for physics to settle.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The letter 'l' should fall straight down, collide with the target, and trigger a level win (score increases, level index increments).

TEST_2.test_description: Verify that round objects roll on slopes.
TEST_2.strategy_description: On Level 2 (target offset to the right with a ramp), type the letter 'o' and start simulation.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The 'o' should land on the ramp, roll to the right due to physics, and hit the target.

TEST_3.strategy_description: Type "oo" to spawn two balls. The first should hit the second or they should pile up.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Both bodies spawn, collide with each other, and physics simulation remains stable without tunneling.
